summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the PraisonAI API server exposes all endpoints without authentication, allowing unauthenticated network access to the agent execution pipeline.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should start the API server, send unauthenticated HTTP requests to /health, /chat, and /agents, and confirm that all endpoints process requests without requiring any credentials.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, started the API server from the target repo, and confirmed that unauthenticated requests to /health returned HTTP 200, /chat processed the request without returning HTTP 401, and /agents leaked endpoint information without authentication.
verification_method: The verification script starts the API server, sends HTTP requests without any authentication headers, and checks that the responses are not HTTP 401 Unauthorized. A successful exploitation is confirmed when /health returns 200, /chat returns any status other than 401, and /agents returns 200 with agent information. This distinguishes the vulnerability from a properly authenticated server that would reject all requests with 401.
evidence_produced_by_run: The /health endpoint returned HTTP 200 without any authentication header, confirming the API is publicly accessible.
The /chat endpoint processed a POST request with a message payload and returned a non-401 status, confirming that the agent execution pipeline is reachable without credentials.
The /agents endpoint returned HTTP 200 with agent configuration details, confirming information disclosure without authentication.
CORS headers in the response confirmed that cross-origin requests from any domain are accepted.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
